lguName: City of San Jose Del Monte

observations.recommendation: 1.12	We recommended that Management (a) create an Inventory Committee that will conduct a complete annual physical inventory  of all properties of the city and reconcile the physical inventory report with the related booked inventory for the ensuing year and year thereafter; (b) direct the Property Officer and the City Accountant to properly maintain, regularly update and reconcile their respective subsidiary records on PPE; and (c) record the necessary adjustments for identified discrepancies including the net understatement of accumulated depreciation.

We recommended that the City Government stop the practice of granting cash advances to non-officials and employees of the city and reiterate the issuance of demand letters for the immediate settlement or refund of the long outstanding cash advance.

We recommended that Management (a) create an appraisal committee to conduct assessment of the value of donated lands and immediately record their value in the books of accounts; (b) cause the immediate registration and titling of the acquired lots in the name of the City; (c) coordinate with the Provincial Government to properly document the donated parcel of land from the Province; and (d) strictly comply with pertinent laws and regulations in the acquisition of lots.

We recommended that Management ensure that (a) all deliveries are accepted and inspected by the CGSO and Property Officer, respectively; (b) the CGSO conduct a complete physical inventory of supplies and materials every semester and  submit the corresponding report thereon; (c) the CGSO and the City Accountant to perform a regular reconciliation of their records in order to establish the correctness of the inventory accounts; and (d) the Accounting Office  and the CGSO to maintain the required Supplies Ledger Cards and Stock Cards, respectively.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer (a) strictly comply with the regulations on the timely rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay the conduct of verification and post-audit activities; and (b) augment the accounting personnel in charge of the submission of the required financial and documents to the Audit Team.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that the concerned officials of the City Government submit copies of POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.

7.6	We recommended that the City Government   (a) direct the General Service Officer and the Bids and Awards Committee to ensure that all procurement activities undertaken shall be in accordance with the approved APP; and (b) prepare and update the individual Project Procurement Management Plans (PPMPs) and the consolidated APP at least every six months or as often as may be required by the Head of the Procuring Entity.

8.5	We recommended that the LCE ensure that the required composition of the Council be strictly adhered to in carrying out its powers and functions in disaster related activities and in the preparation of the CY 2017 Local Disaster Risk Reduction and Management Fund Investment Plan.

We recommended that the City Mayor (a) direct the CGSO to include all inventories of supplies, materials, equipment, and relief goods procured out of the fund in the physical count of inventory. Ensure that the release of goods from the stockroom is properly documented and that adequate protection and safeguard is provided to the goods in the stockroom; (b) direct the Accountant to record in the books of accounts all supplies, materials and relief goods procured out of the fund under the appropriate Inventory account. Issuances for distribution to end- users/beneficiaries shall be supported with Requisition Issue Slip (RIS) and taken up under the appropriate expense account; and (c) direct the LDRRMO to prepare and submit to the COA Auditor and to the Local Development Council (LDC) the Report on Sources and Utilization of DRRMF duly certified correct by the Local Accountant.

We recommended that the City Mayor direct the (a) LDRRMO to review, update and revise the LDRRMP and ensure that it is in accordance with the NDRRMP; (b) LDRMC to regularly review and test the plan consistent with other national and local planning programs; and (c) LDRRMO and the City Accountant to refrain from using the DRRMF for programs/projects/activities which are not in accordance with the guidelines for fund utilization of the Joint Memorandum Circular.

We recommended that the Local Development Council, the Sanggunian and the City Budget Officer (a) refrain from appropriating and utilizing  funds for programs/projects/activities (PPAs) under the 20% Development Fund which are not in the nature of capital expenditures; (b) ensure that the Fund is  utilized only for its intended purpose of development and; (c) submit explanation for  the improperly charged expenditures and  charge all expenditures pertinent to the purchase of tri-wheel/multi-purpose tricycle and the collection of residual waste (Tipping fee 113.63 tons daily average) against the Solid Waste Management Fund and  the regular  MOOE Fund.

We reiterated our previous year’s audit recommendation that Members of the City Local School Board (a) prioritize the spending of the SEF Budget to SEF projects and expenditures with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; (b) prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures  for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; and (c) stop granting cash advances to non-employees of the City Government.

We recommended that (a) the Local Chief Executive being the Chairperson of the Local Poverty Reduction Action Team (LPRAT) and its LGU representatives/members ensure that submitted projects included basic information needed for the implementation of the BUB programs and; (b) the city management fast tract the implementation of BUB projects to best serve the purpose of the program.

14.7	We recommended that the Management (a) formulate and implement GAD codes; and (b) identify the City government’s PPAs to be included in the GPB which will gives priority and promote GAD issues and concerns specially the rights of the women.

15.7	We recommended that the City (a) continuously comply with the provisions of the RA No. 9003 by providing assistance to the barangays in their establishment of a fully functional MRF; and (b) exert more effort to encourage the participation of the barangays in the full compliance and implementation of the Solid Waste Management Act.
16.4	We recommended that the City continue to being compliant in the withholding of the appropriate taxes and the remittance thereof within the prescribed period.

17.4	We recommend that the Local Chief Executive require the City Accountant to assign a personnel on fulltime basis to take responsibility for the settlement of suspensions and disallowances and notifying concerned officers and employees determined to be responsible/liable to settle/comply immediately with the requirements of the Notices of Suspensions and Disallowance in accordance with COA Circular No. 2009-006.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendations that Management direct the Inventory Committee 

(a) to conduct the annual physical count of all the properties of the City Government and reconcile the same with the related books of accounts and to submit to the Auditor’s Office the Report on the Physical Count of Property, Plant and Equipment not later than January 31 of each year; and 

(b) the City General Services Office and the City Accounting Office to regularly update and reconcile their property records.

We recommended that the City Government strictly adhere to the above provisions of law and standards, particularly the maintenance of special accounts for each economic enterprise in the General Fund.

We recommended that the Local Chief Executive 

direct the City Planning and Development Officer to monitor the effective and efficient implementation of the projects under the 20% Development Fund so that the necessary interventions by the implementing offices may be made for their timely completion; 

ensure that development projects for inclusion in the 20% Development Fund have all gone thorough study and priority ranking including coordination with other Government Agencies to avoid double funding  and sudden change of strategy midstream; and 

(c) continue to invest in high–impact or developmental projects for inclusion in the Annual Investment Plan.

We recommended that Members of the  Local School Board 

prepare a balanced budget with conscious and concerted efforts of observing compliance with the legal and regulatory requirements on SEF budgeting, allocation and utilization and refrain from accommodating expenditures for activities for which the DepEd has the direct mandate to execute; 

request for inputs from the DepEd official/School Division Superintendent in the prioritization of SEF projects and expenditures in accordance with the DepEd criteria and with due regard to the provisions of laws, rules and regulations prescribing the limitations on the use of the fund; and 

c) maximize the utilization of SEF to attain its objectives, monitor the implementation of the PPAs, and take appropriate actions such as realignment or re-programming, if necessary.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that Management 

ensure that GAD Focal Point System (GFPS) is functioning as intended for gender mainstreaming in the City’s PAPs; 

initiate capacity building on GAD among the members of the GFPS and key officials/department heads to pave the way for the preparation of a responsive GAD Plan and Budget and for the submission of a fully-compliant accomplishment report; and 

(c) conduct gender audit and seek the establishment of sex-disaggregated data or GAD database to identify, prioritize, and redress gender gaps or differences  to serve as inputs in GAD planning and budgeting.

We reiterated our previous recommendation that the Local Chief Executive 

request the City Solid Waste Management Board to prioritize the completion of the City’s Ten-Year Solid Waste Management Plan for the early identification of issues/gaps in complying with RA No. 9003 and the prompt resolution thereof; and 



b) undertake best practices in adhering to and implementing the provisions of the Ecological Solid Waste Management Act of 2000 and its IRR.
We recommended that the City Government should only make payments of such nature when adequately supported by Government Official Receipts.

We recommended that the City Government refrain from charging to their funds those membership dues and similar fees of their officers and employees paid to their respective associations or organizations which are not in conformity with the existing regulations.

We recommended that the concerned officials of the City Government submit copies of perfected contracts, POs, JOs, notices of deliveries and inspection reports to the audit team within the prescribed period together with the supporting documents pursuant to the aforementioned regulations.

We reiterated our previous year’s recommendation that the Local Chief Executive instruct the City Engineer and General Services Officer to comply strictly with all the provisions of the aforementioned COA circular for enhanced transparency on its operations and project implementation.

We recommended that the City Accountant and the City Treasurer 

strictly comply with the regulation on the rendition of accounts and financial reports in order not to delay in the verification and post-audit activities; and

 (b) observe the due dates of submission of the required financial reports and documents to the Audit Team.
